Write a read_file tool function that safely reads files from disk within WORKING_DIR only. Read WORKING_DIR from environment, raise ValueError if not set, resolve paths to prevent directory traversal by validating target_path is under base_path, return FILE_NOT_FOUND for missing files, return file contents otherwise. Include Google-style docstring with Args, Returns, and security notes.
@tool\ndef read_file(file_path: str) -> str:\n    \

Create a secure file reading tool for agent workflows that reads files only from within WORKING_DIR. Load WORKING_DIR from environment, raise ValueError if missing, resolve both base and target paths to their canonical forms, validate that target is a descendant of base (prevent directory traversal), return FILE_NOT_FOUND for non-existent files, and return file contents with UTF-8 encoding.
@tool\ndef read_file(file_path: str) -> str:\n    \

Implement a path-sandboxed file reader tool that restricts file access to WORKING_DIR. Retrieve WORKING_DIR from environment and raise ValueError if unset, resolve both paths to avoid symlink/traversal attacks, use relative_to() to enforce boundary checking, handle path resolution errors by raising INVALID_PATH, return FILE_NOT_FOUND for missing files, read and return UTF-8 file contents.
@tool\ndef read_file(file_path: str) -> str:\n    \

Design a security-hardened file access tool that constrains reads to WORKING_DIR boundaries. Load WORKING_DIR from environment (raise ValueError if missing), normalize both base and target paths via resolve() to eliminate symlinks, validate containment using relative_to() - raising INVALID_PATH on traversal attempts, gracefully return FILE_NOT_FOUND for non-existent paths, and return decoded UTF-8 file content.
@tool\ndef read_file(file_path: str) -> str:\n    \
